concept: Phase / Stage in a Cycle / Part of Process
description: A distinct period or stage in a process of change or development. 'Kaero' names the segment within cycles—the waxing moon, the sprouting seed, the stage within transformation.
sound_symbolism: The word moves through change. 'Ka' marks with clarity, phase defined. The vowel 'e' opens to transition. Then 'ro' rolls into what comes next, phase shifting.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo kaero' (the phase). Implies part of a larger cycle, unlike 'mosha' (period) which can stand alone.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Recognizing phases prevents panic—this is a phase, it will change, nothing stays fixed.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Moon phases marked time—waxing for planting, waning for harvest, each phase with its appropriate activities.
